seoTitle: How to Delete a Payment in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Remove an incorrect payment entry in QuickBooks Online, and learn when to delete versus void a transaction to keep records accurate.
intro: Deleting a payment in QuickBooks Online is sometimes necessary to correct errors and keep your accounts accurate, but it's important to understand when deleting is the right move versus voiding or editing a transaction instead. This guide covers the process and common scenarios.

steps.heading: Locate the payment to delete
steps.body: Navigate to the transaction list in QuickBooks Online and find the specific payment entry that needs to be removed.

steps.heading: Confirm it should be deleted, not edited or voided
steps.body: Review whether deleting is the appropriate action compared to voiding or simply editing the transaction, since each has different effects on your records.

steps.heading: Delete the payment
steps.body: Use the delete option on the transaction and confirm the action when prompted.

steps.heading: Review the account balance afterward
steps.body: Check that the related invoice or account balance updates correctly once the payment is removed.

steps.heading: Document the reason for deletion
steps.body: Keep a note of why the payment was deleted for your own records or for future reference during a review.

faq.question: What's the difference between deleting and voiding a payment?
faq.answer: Deleting removes the transaction entirely from your records, while voiding keeps a record of the transaction but zeroes out its value — voiding is often preferred for audit trail purposes.

faq.question: Will deleting a payment affect the related invoice status?
faq.answer: Yes, once a payment is deleted, the related invoice typically reverts to an unpaid or partially paid status.

faq.question: Can a deleted payment be recovered?
faq.answer: Deleted transactions are generally not easily recoverable, so it's worth being certain before deleting rather than editing or voiding.

faq.question: When should I delete a payment instead of editing it?
faq.answer: Deleting is usually appropriate when the payment was entered entirely in error, while editing is better suited for correcting details on a valid transaction.
